segments.text: When the day of Pentecost came, they were all together in one place.
Suddenly a sound like the blowing of a violent wind came from heaven and filled the whole house where they were sitting.
They saw what seemed to be tongues of fire that separated and came to rest on each of them.
All of them were filled with the Holy Spirit and began to speak in other tongues as the Spirit enabled them.
Now there were staying in Jerusalem God-fearing Jews from every nation under heaven.
When they heard this sound, a crowd came together in bewilderment, because each one heard their own language being spoken.
Utterly amazed,
Aren't all these who are speaking Galileans?
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: Then how is it that each of us hears them in our native language?
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: Parthians, Medes and Elamites; residents of Mesopotamia, Judea and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia,
Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt and the parts of Libya near Cyrene; visitors from Rome
(both Jews and converts to Judaism); Cretans and Arabs—
we hear them declaring the wonders of God in our own tongues!
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: Amazed and perplexed,
What does this mean?
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: Some, however, made fun of them
They have had too much wine.
segments.speaker: Some

segments.text: Then Peter stood up with the Eleven, raised his voice and addressed the crowd:
Fellow Jews and all of you who live in Jerusalem, let me explain this to you; listen carefully to what I say.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: These people are not drunk, as you suppose. It's only nine in the morning!
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: No, this is what was spoken by the prophet Joel:
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy, your young men will see visions, your old men will dream dreams.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Even on my servants, both men and women, I will pour out my Spirit in those days, and they will prophesy.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will show wonders in the heavens above and signs on the earth below, blood and fire and billows of smoke.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The sun will be turned to darkness and the moon to blood before the coming of the great and glorious day of the Lord.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Fellow Israelites, listen to this: Jesus of Nazareth was a man accredited by God to you by miracles, wonders and signs, which God did among you through him, as you yourselves know.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: This man was handed over to you by God's deliberate plan and foreknowledge; and you, with the help of wicked men, put him to death by nailing him to the cross.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: But God raised him from the dead, freeing him from the agony of death, because it was impossible for death to keep its hold on him.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: David said about him: I saw the Lord always before me. Because he is at my right hand, I will not be shaken.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Therefore my heart is glad and my tongue rejoices; my body also will rest in hope,
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: because you will not abandon me to the realm of the dead, you will not let your holy one see decay.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: You have made known to me the paths of life; you will fill me with joy in your presence.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Fellow Israelites, I can tell you confidently that the patriarch David died and was buried, and his tomb is here to this day.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: But he was a prophet and knew that God had promised him on oath that he would place one of his descendants on his throne.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Seeing what was to come, he spoke of the resurrection of the Messiah, that he was not abandoned to the realm of the dead, nor did his body see decay.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: God has raised this Jesus to life, and we are all witnesses of it.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Exalted to the right hand of God, he has received from the Father the promised Holy Spirit and has poured out what you now see and hear.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: For David did not ascend to heaven, and yet he said, The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right hand
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Therefore let all Israel be assured of this: God has made this Jesus, whom you crucified, both Lord and Messiah.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: When the people heard this, they were cut to the heart
Brothers, what shall we do?
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: Repent and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins. And you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: The promise is for you and your children and for all who are far off—for all whom the Lord our God will call.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: With many other words he warned them; and he pleaded with them, Save yourselves from this corrupt generation.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Those who accepted his message were baptized, and about three thousand were added to their number that day.
They devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and to fellowship, to the breaking of bread and to prayer.
Everyone was filled with awe at the many wonders and signs performed by the apostles.
All the believers were together and had everything in common.
They sold property and possessions to give to anyone who had need.
Every day they continued to meet together in the temple courts. They broke bread in their homes and ate together with glad and sincere hearts,
praising God and enjoying the favor of all the people. And the Lord added to their number daily those who were being saved.
